burial.plot_notes: Chapel Hill Cemetery, Parishville, St. Lawrence County, NY

cross_connections.display_label: Two Cyprian Harts, both adopted by Gen. Selah Hart
cross_connections.notes: H00568 (this entry) and H00562 both bore the name Cyprian Hart and both were documented as adopted heirs of General Selah Hart (H00273) — a striking naming + adoption coincidence.

cross_connections.display_label: owned the New Britain mill Cyprian ran

bio.bio_blurb: New Britain miller, later of Parishville

narrative_blocks.header: He ran Deacon Elijah Hart's mill
narrative_blocks.body: A miller, Cyprian Hart came to New Britain to run Deacon Elijah Hart's mill and raised his family beside it before removing to Parishville, New York.

career.organization: Deacon Elijah Hart's mill, New Britain

bio.bio_blurb: Blacksmith and ax-maker, tall and active

career.notes: Carried on ax-making at Burlington, Conn.

narrative_blocks.body: Ordelia died in 1817, in her first year.

narrative_blocks.body: Harriet died in 1828 at the age of nine.

sources.short: Andrews, Genealogical History of Deacon Stephen Hart (1875)

bio.bio_blurb: Connecticut-born farmer who settled in Iowa

narrative_blocks.header: He farmed his way west to Iowa
narrative_blocks.body: Born in Connecticut and raised at Parishville, New York, George Lewis Hart followed the frontier to Cerro Gordo and Crawford counties, Iowa, where he died in 1893.

sources.short: Andrews, Genealogical History of Deacon Stephen Hart (1875)

naming_inspiration.description: Named for his paternal grandfather Lt. Noadiah Hart of Kensington.

career.notes: Learned the clothier's trade of Moses D. Seymour.

narrative_blocks.body: Percy died in 1809 at the age of three. His sister Ardelia died within weeks — the family likely lost both children to one illness.

sources.short: Andrews, Genealogical History of Deacon Stephen Hart (1875)

narrative_blocks.header: Buried in one grave with her brother
narrative_blocks.body: Ardelia died at one year old on April 4, 1809, a day after her four-year-old brother Percy, and the two were laid in a single grave.
